Yesterday, I walked for hours.
We cooked dinner together.
I went to the mountains.
We saw a waterfall.
So, how was your weekend? Did you do anything special?
Yes! I took a trip to the coast. I went with my sister.
Nice! What did you do there?
We walked on the beach, and we had fresh fish for lunch. It was a memorable day.
That sounds lovely. Did anything surprise you?
Actually, yes. We saw dolphins! I did not expect that. I realized how much I needed a break.
What a great story. You really needed that trip.
I did. It was the best decision of the month.
Last year, I went on a memorable trip.
First we walked, then we had lunch by the lake.
I did not expect it, and it was a real surprise.
Did you have a good time? Yes, I really did.
Finally, I realized how much I needed that break.

Let me tell you about a trip I took last year. In March, I went to the mountains with two old friends. We rented a small house near a lake. On the first day, the weather was terrible. It rained all afternoon, so we just cooked, talked, and played cards. But on the second day, everything changed. The sky was clear, and we walked for hours. We saw a waterfall, and we had lunch on a big rock. I took hundreds of photos. On the last night, we made a fire and we told old stories until very late. It was simple, but it was one of the best trips of my life. I still remember it clearly.

I want to tell you about a small surprise that made my week. Last Tuesday, I had a very long and difficult day at work. Nothing went right. When I finally got home, I felt tired and a little sad. But when I opened the door, the lights were on, and my family was there. They made my favorite dinner, and there was a cake on the table. I did not expect anything. It was not my birthday. They just wanted to say thank you for a hard year. I almost cried. I realized that a small thing, at the right moment, can change everything. That night, I went to bed happy.
First, I woke up late. Then, I missed the bus. After that, a kind stranger helped me. Finally, I arrived at work on time. At the end, I felt very lucky.
